description: Python object serialization. Converts Python objects to a byte stream (pickling) and back (unpickling). The pickle module implements a fundamental algorithm for serializing and de-serializing Python object structures.

functions.name: dump

params.name: file
params.description: An open file object (or any object with a write() method accepting a string argument). Must be opened in binary mode.

params.name: protocol
params.description: The pickle protocol version (0, 1, or 2). Default is 0 (ASCII). Protocol 2 is the most efficient for Python 2.

returns.description: No return value. The serialized representation is written to the file.

functions.description: Write a pickled representation of obj to the open file object file. Equivalent to Pickler(file, protocol).dump(obj).

functions.name: dumps

params.name: protocol
params.description: The pickle protocol version (0, 1, or 2). Default is 0 (ASCII).

returns.description: The pickled representation of the object as a string

functions.description: Return the pickled representation of the object as a string, instead of writing it to a file.

functions.name: load

params.name: file
params.description: An open file object (or any object with read() and readline() methods). Must be opened in binary mode.

functions.description: Read a pickled object from the open file object file and return the reconstituted object. Equivalent to Unpickler(file).load(). WARNING: pickle is not secure against erroneous or maliciously constructed data.

functions.name: loads
functions.description: Read a pickled object from a string and return the reconstituted object. WARNING: pickle is not secure against erroneous or maliciously constructed data.

classes.name: Pickler
classes.description: Serializes Python objects to a file. Takes a file-like object for writing the pickled data stream.

params.name: file
params.description: An open file object with a write() method that accepts a string argument

params.name: protocol
params.description: The pickle protocol version (0, 1, or 2). Default is 0 (ASCII).

constructors.description: Create a Pickler that writes to the given file object.

methods.name: dump
methods.description: Write a pickled representation of obj to the open file object given in the constructor.

methods.name: clear_memo
methods.description: Clears the pickler's memo, a data structure that remembers which objects have already been pickled. Useful when reusing a Pickler to pickle multiple objects.

classes.name: Unpickler
classes.description: Deserializes Python objects from a file. Takes a file-like object for reading the pickled data stream.

params.name: file
params.description: An open file object with read() and readline() methods

constructors.description: Create an Unpickler that reads from the given file object.

methods.name: load
methods.description: Read a pickled object representation from the open file object given in the constructor and return the reconstituted object.

constants.name: HIGHEST_PROTOCOL
constants.description: The highest protocol version available (2 in Python 2.7). Pass this to Pickler or dump() to use the most efficient protocol.
